sentence: Identify non-fluoropolymer thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing with an application/service temperature of up to 180 °C and a shrink temperature under 250 °C. Additionally, evaluate whether specific material formulations or processing approaches could help meet these performance requirements.

required: Key material attributes for a non‑fluoropolymer heat‑shrink tubing that can replace FEP:
Good adhesion to metal / polymer substrates
Compatibility with electronic packaging standards (ASTM D4283)

extracted_keywords: Key material attributes for a non‑fluoropolymer heat‑shrink tubing that can replace FEP:
Good adhesion to metal / polymer substrates
Compatibility with electronic packaging standards (ASTM D4283)
extracted_constraints: Hard Constraints for the Substitute Heat‑Shrink Material
Continuous Service Temperature – The material must be able to operate continuously at temperatures up to 180 °C without degradation of mechanical or shrinkage performance.
Shrink Temperature – The material must reach its shrinkage point when heated to ≤250 °C (i.e., it must begin to shrink at or below 250 °C).
Flammability – The material must achieve a UL 94 V‑0 flammability rating (or equivalent).
PFAS Exclusion – The material must contain no per‑fluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).

VOC/Regulatory Compliance – The material must comply with VOC emission limits and regulatory requirements such as REACH (EU), TSCA (US), RoHS, and ASTM D 7179 (electronic/medical housing) or equivalent standards.
